DecideRequest.description:
Operator's decision on one proposal.

``rationale`` is optional but encouraged — Phase 7-E's audit trail
is the same ledger that Phase 7-A laid down; an unexplained
rejection is a near-future regret. The UI should default-prompt.
DecideRequest.title: DecideRequest

ProposalView.description:
One proposal as it appears on the wire.

Single-user-mode drops ``owner_id`` + ``workspace_id`` — they're
sentinel UUIDs on every row and add noise. Phase 9+ multi-tenant
work re-surfaces them. ``decided`` is a convenience flag so
dashboards can render decision status without joining client-side.
ProposalView.title: ProposalView

RunSummaryResponse.description: One row from the pipeline_runs ledger as the dashboard sees it.
RunSummaryResponse.title: RunSummaryResponse

TriggerBody.description: v2 trigger request — extensible by ``type`` (correlation_analysis today).
TriggerBody.title: TriggerBody

post.description:
Receive a metric batch from HealthSave iOS app.

Expected payload:
{
    "metric": "heart_rate",
    "batch_index": 0,
    "total_batches": 1,
    "samples": [
        {"date": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "qty": 72, "source": "Apple Watch"},
        ...
    ]
}

get.description: Return record counts so the iOS app knows what's synced.

Return recent anomaly findings from the analysis engine.

Reads ``analysis_findings`` where ``finding_type='anomaly'``, ordered
by ``created_at DESC``. Optional ``since`` limits rows to those
created at-or-after the timestamp. Optional ``severity`` is a
comma-separated list (``info,watch,alert``) matched against the
finding's severity column. SQL lives in
``storage.timescale.briefings`` — this handler does parameter
validation and wire-shape mapping only.
Return today's daily briefing narrative.
Return the most recent daily briefing + weekly summary narratives.

Recent rows from the pipeline_runs ledger.

The ledger is written by the ``apps/worker`` APScheduler listener
(Phase 4B). This route is the read-side surface — newest first,
optional ``job_kind`` filter, capped at 200 per request.

Status values: pending, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled,
skipped. ``triggered_by`` is one of: scheduler, manual, api, event.

parameters.description: Filter to a specific scheduler job (e.g. 'daily_briefing').
parameters.name: job_kind

schema.description: Filter to a specific scheduler job (e.g. 'daily_briefing').
schema.title: Job Kind

get.description:
Return recent trend findings from the analysis engine.

SQL lives in ``storage.timescale.briefings`` — this handler does
parameter validation (period format) and wire-shape mapping only.

parameters.description: Optional day period filter such as 30d or 90d
parameters.name: period

schema.description: Optional day period filter such as 30d or 90d
schema.title: Period

post.description:
Run an ad-hoc analysis job inline.

``daily_briefing`` and ``trend_analysis`` run synchronously against
``app.state.analysis_engine``. Future long-running job types
(weekly, correlation, etc.) should dispatch to the ``apps/worker``
service via a Postgres NOTIFY queue rather than running inline —
the API process no longer carries a scheduler in v2.

get.description: Return the current week's summary narrative.

List recent proposals.

Reads through :func:`storage.timescale.agents.fetch_recent_proposals`
— the Phase 7-B repository owns the SQL (default newest-first via
the supplied ORDER BY). Per the Phase 5 storage-zone rule, the
route never composes its own query against ``action_proposals``.

parameters.description: When true, only return proposals without a matching action_decisions row. The dashboard's review queue uses this; full audit views set false.
parameters.name: undecided_only

schema.description: When true, only return proposals without a matching action_decisions row. The dashboard's review queue uses this; full audit views set false.
schema.title: Undecided Only

post.description:
Record the operator's decision on one proposal.

Writes a single row into ``action_decisions`` via
:func:`storage.timescale.agents.decide_action`. The decision is
append-only — re-deciding (e.g. flipping a rejection to an
approval) writes a new row, and downstream readers take the
newest. The supervisor / executor path that picks up an approved
proposal is Phase 7-F territory; Phase 7-E only persists the
decision.

Errors:

  * ``404`` — no proposal with that id. We intentionally don't
    leak whether the id is malformed-but-syntactically-valid vs.
    truly absent; both surface as 404.

get.description:
Ranked correlation candidates annotated with experiment readiness.

Strongest correlations first — the top of the list is the best candidate to
promote into an experiment. Deduped to one row per metric pair (the strongest
seen), since the same pair recurs across correlation runs.

schema.title: Response List Candidates Api V2 Experiments Candidates Get

get.description: Persisted cross-metric correlation findings, newest first.

parameters.description: Optional day window such as 30d or 90d
parameters.name: period

schema.description: Optional day window such as 30d or 90d
schema.title: Period
Response List Correlations Api V2 Insights Correlations Get

get.description:
Recent structured findings (the evidence feed), newest first.

Each row carries its ``structured_data`` so the evidence card can show the
calculation behind a finding (effect size, window, p-value, …). Optional
``?type=`` narrows to one kind.

parameters.description: Optional finding kind (anomaly / trend / correlation / summary).
parameters.name: type

schema.description: Optional finding kind (anomaly / trend / correlation / summary).
schema.title: Type
Response List Findings Api V2 Insights Findings Get

get.description:
Return a no-secret setup fingerprint for humans and clients.

This is intentionally unauthenticated: it exposes no health data and helps
users distinguish the Data Hub API from nearby Grafana/Homepage ports before
they troubleshoot keys or sync.

schema.title: Response Setup Diagnostics Api V2 Setup Diagnostics Get
